name: config
context: Define repeatable SSH host settings with Host blocks, ProxyJump, identities, and multiplexing
info: OpenSSH client configuration is the force multiplier that turns fragile command-line flags into stable automation. This node covers `~/.ssh/config` and `/etc/ssh/ssh_config`, including `Host` and `Match` blocks, `IdentityFile`, `ProxyJump`, `StrictHostKeyChecking`, `UserKnownHostsFile`, `BatchMode`, keepalives, and connection sharing with `ControlMaster`, `ControlPath`, and `ControlPersist`. Agents use it to normalize host aliases, bastions, keepalive behavior, and tunnel defaults across repeated workflows.
body:
# SSH Client Config

OpenSSH reads per-user config from `~/.ssh/config` and system config from `/etc/ssh/ssh_config`. Put stable hostnames, usernames, identities, bastions, and keepalives here so commands stay short and deterministic.

## Host Blocks

```sshconfig
Include ~/.ssh/config.d/*

Host bastion
  HostName bastion.example.com
  User ops
  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
  AddKeysToAgent yes

Host prod-*
  User deploy
  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
  ServerAliveInterval 15
  ServerAliveCountMax 3
  StrictHostKeyChecking ask
```

`Host` patterns match aliases and real hostnames. `Include` lets you split large configs into smaller files.

## Jump Hosts and Automation Defaults

```sshconfig
Host db-internal
  HostName 10.0.2.15
  ProxyJump bastion
  BatchMode yes
  IdentitiesOnly yes
```

`ProxyJump` is the config equivalent of `ssh -J`. `BatchMode yes` disables password prompts, which is safer for automation because failures become explicit instead of hanging.

## Multiplexing

```sshconfig
Host *
  ControlMaster auto
  ControlPath ~/.ssh/cm-%r@%h:%p
  ControlPersist 10m
```

Connection sharing reuses a master TCP session so later `ssh`, `scp`, and `sftp` commands connect faster. Use `ssh -O check host` to inspect the master and `ssh -O exit host` to stop it.

sections.title: Host and Match blocks
sections.body: `Host` defines patterns that set per-target defaults like `User`, `HostName`, `Port`, and `IdentityFile`. `Match` applies settings conditionally based on user, host, or tags. Use `ssh -G host` to print the evaluated config after `Host` and `Match` processing.

sections.title: ProxyJump and bastions
sections.body: `ProxyJump` is the persistent form of `ssh -J`. It lets a client reach private hosts through one or more bastions. Command-line options generally apply to the final destination rather than the jump hosts, so use config files when bastions need their own identities or options. See [[ssh/connect]].

sections.title: Connection multiplexing
sections.body: `ControlMaster`, `ControlPath`, and `ControlPersist` let later sessions reuse one authenticated connection. This reduces repeated handshakes for `ssh`, `scp`, and `sftp`. Active masters are controlled with `ssh -O check`, `ssh -O exit`, or `ssh -O forward`. Multiplexing is especially useful before bursts of transfers in [[ssh/copy]] or repeated commands in [[ssh/connect]].

links.context: Config settings shape interactive and batch SSH execution
scp and sftp inherit SSH config including bastions and identities
Forwarding can be defined with LocalForward and RemoteForward
AddKeysToAgent and IdentityAgent affect agent-backed auth
StrictHostKeyChecking and UserKnownHostsFile define host verification behavior
Use ssh -G and verbose logs to debug config resolution

related.note: Config turns one-off SSH commands into repeatable host workflows
LocalForward, RemoteForward, and DynamicForward can live in config
Identity loading and agent behavior are often configured here
